hacks.title: Chromoscope
hacks.description: A web-based tool to explore the sky across the electromagnetic spectrum, from gamma rays to radio waves. Runs in any browser with no plugins. Could also run offline from a USB stick. Launched at .Astronomy 2 and covered by Universe Today.

hacks.title: GLIMPSE Viewer
hacks.description: A viewer for the Spitzer GLIMPSE survey, showing the Milky Way in infrared. Demonstrated at .Astronomy 2 alongside the Adler Planetarium's world's largest Milky Way image.

hacks.title: Pluto the Previous Planet
hacks.description: A song about Pluto's demotion from planetary status, performed by Amanda Bauer and collaborators at the hack day. Covered by Universe Today.

hacks.title: Chromotone
hacks.description: Exploring multiwavelength astronomy with sound. Each waveband has a pitch; the intensity/brightness in that waveband is represented by the volume of a note. When you scan a mouse over an image, Chromotone plays a chord with components corresponding to the intensity at each pixel. This .Astronomy 3 version aimed to make a lighter, more web-friendly version of the original Processing implementation, with added functionality.

hacks.title: AstroScanr
hacks.description: A visualisation of astronomy journal titles and abstracts using natural language processing techniques.

hacks.title: Astroworld
hacks.description: Collect information about astronomical facilities around the world, including old, abandoned observatories, flashy new ones, future ones, and archeo-astronomical sites, using EpiCollect.

hacks.title: Cosmology Calculator OS X Widget
hacks.description: A port of Ned Wright's Cosmology Calculator to an OS X widget.

hacks.title: Flora
hacks.description: An attempt to enable quantitative measurements on images in a browser, measuring the position, size, and brightness of objects in astronomy images. Uses the astrojs library for FITS images and coordinate transformations.

hacks.title: Science: It's Universal
hacks.description: An outreach video about what science is and what it is like to be a scientist.

hacks.title: Sh*t Scientists Say
hacks.description: A collection of what astronomers love, hate, and repeat.

hacks.title: Zoonibot
hacks.description: A "Planet Hunters butler" for the Zooniverse Talk platform, ready to automatically answer questions and provide detail when users request information.

hacks.title: Don't Call Me Colin
hacks.description: The International Astronomical Union announced that extrasolar planets might get more interesting names. This hack is about a planet (Gliese 581d) that really wants a nice mythical name like the Solar System planets, and not to be named after a celebrity or someone's cat (Colin).

hacks.title: D3PO
hacks.description: D3PO is designed to allow an astronomer, with no specialised data visualisation skills, to make an interactive, publication-quality figure with staged builds and linked brushing across plots.

hacks.title: Python Client for the NASA ADS API
hacks.description: A Python module to interact with NASA's ADS that Doesn't Suck.

hacks.title: AAS Job Viz
hacks.description: A visualisation of the AAS job register using location to pin jobs on a map. Allows users to filter jobs by type and location, using the Leaflet.js JavaScript library.

hacks.title: Adler Zodiac Story
hacks.description: Online storytelling connecting the Chinese Zodiac to astronomy.

hacks.title: Astronomer Bot (BotAstro)
hacks.description: A Twitter bot for astronomers.

hacks.title: Cite Me Maybe
hacks.description: See which articles were referenced in your "field" that a paper did not cite.

hacks.title: Elevate ApJ
hacks.description: Working to elevate the readability and design of astronomy journal articles.

hacks.title: Kepler Song and Drum Machine
hacks.description: Making music from Kepler light curves.

hacks.title: Rate My Institution
hacks.description: A way to honestly rate your place of academic work.

hacks.title: Tones and Drones: Sampling Stars
hacks.description: Sonifying Kepler light curves to create music.

hacks.title: Unclockify
hacks.description: Chrome extension to find and replace sexagesimal coordinates on Wikipedia.

hacks.title: Life Cycle of Astronomers
hacks.description: Visualising astronomy career paths with D3.js.

hacks.title: Friendly Virtual Radio Interferometer
hacks.description: A friendly virtual radio interferometer.

hacks.title: Literal Virtual Observatory
hacks.description: Drive around telescope observatories in a Mad Max/gamer-style interactive visualisation.

hacks.title: Pudding to the Max
hacks.description: Catch up with the exploits of the Cosmic Pudding at .Astronomy through a Koalas-to-the-Max style interface.

hacks.title: Radio Yourself
hacks.description: Upload an image and see what you'd look like through a radio telescope.

hacks.title: robo-ph
hacks.description: A podcast that reads astro-ph abstracts one by one.

hacks.title: Space Shark
hacks.description: An alt-az pointer to be used for education.

hacks.title: webcite
hacks.description: Takes an ADS bibliographic code and plots a geographical web of citations for that paper. Finds the institutions of first authors of papers that cite the given paper.

hacks.title: What's Up?
hacks.description: Updated Astroplan web app to help you plan observations.

hacks.title: .draft
hacks.description: Automagically highlight LaTeX differences between GitHub drafts.

hacks.title: StingRA: Simbad Quick-Look Chrome Extension
hacks.description: A Chrome extension that lets astronomers highlight an astronomical object and open a quick-look pop-up of magnitudes, images, sky position, and links to SIMBAD and WorldWide Telescope.

hacks.title: Are You Still Talking?
hacks.description: Use a blink(1) light to control your speakers when chairing a session. A Python package that installs a command-line talk timer, green while talking, orange in the warning period, red when time's up.

hacks.title: arxiv59
hacks.description: Twitter bot that posts papers which missed the top-of-the-arXiv deadline by about a second.

hacks.title: The ArXiv Mailer
hacks.description: An app to modernise the formatting of the daily arXiv email.

hacks.title: AstroBadger
hacks.description: Getting you in the habit of writing 200 academic words per day, every day. Gamified writing! Chrome plugin plus Flask app.

hacks.title: AstroGoldStars
hacks.description: Give a Gold Star to a fellow scientist by tweeting @username++ at @AstroGoldStars on Twitter. Solves credit sharing in science.

hacks.title: bvi-sonification
hacks.description: Uses a Leap Motion device to allow a user to listen to a galaxy's spectra based on hand position.

hacks.title: Fakespeare
hacks.description: A LSTM neural network trained on the corpus of all Shakespeare plays that can generate hilarious and nonsensical plays.

hacks.title: Galaxy Zoo Maze
hacks.description: Creates a responsive view of a Zooniverse subject and a QR code, then supports scanning and reacting to results.

hacks.title: GitNL
hacks.description: Run Git commands using normal English sentences parsed with Google's natural language processor.

hacks.title: Hacks Collector
hacks.description: A way to archive all hacks made at .Astronomy conferences. Each hack has a separate YAML file containing all metadata.

hacks.title: Markov Papers
hacks.description: Generates paper titles/abstracts with a Markov chain trained on real papers returned by any ADS search keywords.

hacks.title: Meta^4hack
hacks.description: Metadata on a hack about metadata on hacks, the submission form for this hack list.

hacks.title: Project Apollo Stories
hacks.description: A Zooniverse Project to re-create incredible stories from the Apollo missions using selected images from the Apollo Project Flickr gallery and Timeline JS.

hacks.title: SIMBAD the Bot
hacks.description: Twitter bot that tweets a multi-wavelength animated GIF of a random SIMBAD object a few times a day.

hacks.title: Spacebar
hacks.description: Astronomy-themed mini-games where you only need the spacebar. Keep rain out of the dome while observing, find the galaxies, and more.

hacks.title: Stellar Autopsies Zooniverse
hacks.description: A Zooniverse Project to classify the light curves of dying stars.

hacks.title: Hack Graveyard
hacks.description: .Astronomy is full of amazing pitches and hacks. Unfortunately, there are never enough hours in the Hack Day to complete all of them. The Hack Graveyard tracks these incomplete hacks so they can be revived in the future.

hacks.title: Hack Roulette
hacks.description: Spin the wheel to generate a new hack using past dotastro hacks.

hacks.title: Project PHaEDRA Website and Logo
hacks.description: Rethinking and rough drafting a logo and website for Project PHaEDRA: a collaborative effort to digitize and transcribe the work of early astronomers.

hacks.title: Sketch dotAstro: Hunting for Jupiter Sketches in Archival Notebooks
hacks.description: Attempting to use computer vision to distinguish between boring pages in archival notebooks and pages with sketches of Jupiter.

hacks.title: Wave At Kepler
hacks.description: Static website to compute the direction to Kepler when it takes a photo, so you can wave at the right moment.

hacks.title: Where in the Universe?
hacks.description: Have you ever wondered where you'd be in the Universe if you were launched toward the zenith above your birth place at the time you were born? This site calculates your position in space and the nearest star.

hacks.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
hacks.description: A small package for plotting common spectral line lists on your spectra, so you can discover which absorption/emission features are responsible for what you see.

hacks.title: xorastro: Extremely Open, Extremely Reproducible Astronomy
hacks.description: Built a neural network to predict galaxy colors from galaxy shapes in the most open and reproducible way possible.

hacks.title: Contributor Plots for Astropy-Affiliated Packages
hacks.description: Visualisation tools showing contribution activity across the Astropy ecosystem of affiliated packages.

hacks.title: Data Sonification
hacks.description: Tools and techniques for converting astronomical data into sound.

hacks.title: Specreduce
hacks.description: A spectral data reduction package for Astropy.

hacks.title: Social Justice Reading Group Resource
hacks.description: A curated resource to support astronomy departments running social justice and equity reading groups.

hacks.title: Interactive Research Explorer
hacks.description: A public outreach tool for exploring research interactively.

hacks.title: .Astronomy X Scripts
hacks.description: Scripts and code developed at .Astronomy X.

hacks.title: .Astronomy X Resources
hacks.description: Repository for information and materials from .Astronomy X.

hacks.title: Ru-stronomy
hacks.description: A Markov chain generator trained on drag queen names, astronomical acronyms, RuPaul lyrics, and clickbait science headlines. Generates new astronomical mission names that sound like drag queens -- and vice versa. Created as part of the "Engaging astronomers in the era of clickbait" talk.

hacks.title: Contributing to Astropy
hacks.description: A session on how to contribute to the Astropy project.

hacks.title: Snakemake and Show Your Work
hacks.description: A tutorial session on reproducible workflows using Snakemake and the showyourwork framework.

hacks.title: Multi-Order Coverage (MOC) Hack
hacks.description: Working with Multi-Order Coverage maps for astronomical footprints in MAST.

hacks.title: dotAstro Demo
hacks.description: Demo materials from .Astronomy 12.

hacks.title: Promoting Bluesky to Astronomers
hacks.description: A live feed project connected to the conference, promoting Bluesky as a social platform for astronomers.

hacks.title: Website for Astronomy Illustrations
hacks.description: A website for sharing and discovering astronomy illustrations.

hacks.title: DumBot
hacks.description: A bot project from .Astronomy 13.

hacks.title: Galaxy Sonification (+ VR maybe)
hacks.description: Converting astronomical data about galaxies into sound, with a possible VR component.

hacks.title: Datacube Listener, Live!
hacks.description: Live listening to astronomical datacubes.

hacks.title: dotAstronomy13 Soundtrack
hacks.description: Creating a musical soundtrack for the conference.

hacks.title: Astronomy as a Great Tool to Have Impact on Humans in 21st Century
hacks.description: Exploring astronomy as a tool for broader human impact in the 21st century.

hacks.title: Astrotalks: ArXiV for Presentations
hacks.description: A platform for sharing and discovering astronomy presentations, inspired by arXiv.

hacks.title: Make a Search Bar
hacks.description: Building a search bar for astronomical data.

hacks.title: AstroCollab
hacks.description: A collaboration tool for astronomers.

hacks.title: Set up JDaviz Example Notebooks on Datalabs
hacks.description: Creating example notebooks using JDaviz on the ESA Datalabs platform.

hacks.title: So You Want to Be an Astropy Contributor?
hacks.description: A session on getting started with contributing to the Astropy project.

hacks.title: Redo the Software Use in Astro Survey
hacks.description: Updating a survey of software usage in the astronomy community.

hacks.title: Getting Started with ML in Astronomy
hacks.description: An introductory session on machine learning for astronomers.

hacks.title: Adding to the ESASky Virtual Assistant
hacks.description: Extending the ESASky virtual assistant with new capabilities.

hacks.title: Astro Careers, Networks, Publication Stats
hacks.description: Exploring astronomy career networks and publication statistics.

hacks.title: How to Tailor Outreach Projects for Marginalised Communities
hacks.description: Exploring approaches for tailoring astronomy outreach to marginalised communities.
